lambda.name: AWS Lambda
lambda.extraction_focus: Locate the 'Supported runtimes' or 'Deprecated runtimes' tables. Focus on the most important deprecation or block items from 2024 and later. For each runtime, extract: runtime name, runtime identifier, operating system, deprecation_date, block_create_date, and block_update_date.

eks.name: Amazon EKS
eks.extraction_focus: Locate the 'Amazon EKS Kubernetes release calendar' table or similar. Extract all rows from this table. For each Kubernetes version, extract: version number, upstream release date, EKS release date, end of standard support date, and end of extended support date.

rds.name: Amazon RDS
rds.extraction_focus: Locate database engine version lifecycle tables (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL version tables). Extract all rows from these tables. For each engine version, extract available fields such as: major version, community release date, RDS release date, community end of life date, RDS end of standard support date, RDS start of Extended Support year 1 pricing date, RDS start of Extended Support year 3 pricing date. Include all versions regardless of support status.

elasticache.name: Amazon ElastiCache
elasticache.extraction_focus: Locate the 'Extended support and End of Life schedule' table or similar. Extract all rows from this table. For each Redis OSS version, extract: Major Engine Version, End of Standard Support, Start of Extended Support Y1 Premium, Start of Extended Support Y2 Premium, Start of Extended Support Y3 Premium, End of Extended Support and version EOL. Use consistent field names: end_of_standard_support_date, start_extended_support_y1_date, start_extended_support_y2_date, start_extended_support_y3_date, end_of_extended_support_date.

item_properties.name: Redis OSS version with status (e.g., 'Redis OSS v4 (Extended Support)')
item_properties.start_extended_support_y1_date: Start of Extended Support Year 1 Premium
item_properties.start_extended_support_y2_date: Start of Extended Support Year 2 Premium
item_properties.start_extended_support_y3_date: Start of Extended Support Year 3 Premium
item_properties.end_of_extended_support_date: End of Extended Support and version EOL date

opensearch.name: Amazon OpenSearch Service
opensearch.extraction_focus: Locate the 'Standard and extended support' tables or similar. CRITICAL FILTERING RULE: COMPLETELY IGNORE and DO NOT EXTRACT any row where 'End of Standard Support' OR 'End of Extended Support' contains 'Not announced'. ONLY extract rows that have actual dates in BOTH support columns (e.g., 'November 7, 2025', 'November 7, 2026'). Skip versions like 'OpenSearch 2.11 and higher versions', 'OpenSearch 1.3', 'Elasticsearch 7.9' that show 'Not announced' in their support dates. For each valid version with announced dates, extract: Software Version, End of Standard Support date, End of Extended Support date.

item_properties.name: Search engine and version (e.g., 'OpenSearch 1.3', 'Elasticsearch 7.10')
item_properties.end_of_standard_support_date: Date when standard support ends (must be actual date, not 'Not announced')
item_properties.end_of_extended_support_date: Date when extended support ends (must be actual date, not 'Not announced')

elasticbeanstalk.name: AWS Elastic Beanstalk
elasticbeanstalk.extraction_focus: Locate the 'Retiring' or 'Retired' tables or similar. Focus on the most important retirement items from 2025 and later. For each platform branch, extract: Runtime version, Platform branch name, Operating system, and retirement dates (both target retirement dates and actual retirement dates).

item_properties.name: Full platform branch name (e.g., 'Corretto 17 AL2', 'PHP 8.1 AL2023')

bedrock.name: Amazon Bedrock
bedrock.extraction_focus: Locate the 'Legacy and end-of-life (EOL) models' table. Extract ALL rows from this table. For each model, extract: model provider, model name, model ID, available regions, legacy date, EOL date, and public extended access start date. If a field contains a dash or is empty, set it to null. Include all models regardless of provider (Amazon, Anthropic, Meta, Cohere, Mistral, etc.).

msk.name: Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka)
msk.extraction_focus:
Extract Apache Kafka version information from the 'Supported Apache Kafka versions' table.

FILTERING RULES:
- EXCLUDE any row where the 'End of support date' column contains '--'
- EXCLUDE any row where the 'End of support date' starts with '2024' or earlier
- ONLY INCLUDE rows where 'End of support date' starts with '2025' or higher year numbers
- Extract all versions that meet these criteria (the number may vary as AWS adds new versions)

ecs.name: Amazon ECS
ecs.extraction_focus: Locate the Fargate platform versions table. Extract all platform versions with their release dates and deprecation status. For each platform version, extract: version number, release date, deprecation date, and status.

batch.name: AWS Batch
batch.extraction_focus: Locate compute environment platform version information. Extract platform versions and their lifecycle status. For each version, extract: platform version, supported features, deprecation notices, and end of support dates.

item_properties.name: AWS Batch platform version (e.g., 'Batch on Fargate 1.4.0')

aurora.name: Amazon Aurora
aurora.extraction_focus: Locate the Aurora engine version lifecycle tables for both MySQL-compatible and PostgreSQL-compatible editions. Extract all versions with their lifecycle dates. For each version, extract: engine type, version number, release date, end of standard support date, and end of extended support date.

documentdb.name: Amazon DocumentDB
documentdb.extraction_focus: Locate DocumentDB engine version information and lifecycle dates. Extract all engine versions with their release and end of life dates. For each version, extract: engine version, compatibility, release date, and end of life date.

neptune.name: Amazon Neptune
neptune.extraction_focus: Locate Neptune engine version lifecycle information. Extract all engine versions with their support status. For each version, extract: engine version, release date, end of life date, and support status.

memorydb.name: Amazon MemoryDB
memorydb.extraction_focus: Locate MemoryDB engine version lifecycle information. Extract all Redis OSS versions supported by MemoryDB with their lifecycle dates. For each version, extract: engine version, release date, end of support date, and status.

dynamodb.name: Amazon DynamoDB
dynamodb.extraction_focus: Locate information about deprecated DynamoDB features such as Global Tables Version 2017 and DynamoDB Streams legacy features. Extract deprecated features with their end of support dates and migration paths.

apigateway.name: Amazon API Gateway
apigateway.extraction_focus: Locate API Gateway version and protocol deprecation information. Extract deprecated API versions, TLS protocol changes, and endpoint type migrations. For each item, extract: feature name, deprecation date, end of support date, and migration instructions.

item_properties.name: API Gateway feature or version (e.g., 'REST API edge-optimized endpoint')

cloudfront.name: Amazon CloudFront
cloudfront.extraction_focus: Locate Lambda@Edge and CloudFront Functions runtime version lifecycle information. Extract deprecated or retiring runtimes for Lambda@Edge. For each runtime, extract: runtime name, version, deprecation date, and end of support date.

appmesh.name: AWS App Mesh
appmesh.extraction_focus: Locate Envoy proxy version lifecycle information used by App Mesh. Extract all Envoy versions with their support status and end of life dates. For each version, extract: Envoy version, release date, end of support date, and status.

emr.name: Amazon EMR
emr.extraction_focus: Locate EMR release version lifecycle information. Extract EMR releases with their lifecycle status, particularly those approaching end of support. For each release, extract: EMR release label, release date, included component versions, and support status.

glue.name: AWS Glue
glue.extraction_focus: Locate AWS Glue version support policy and lifecycle information. Extract all Glue versions with their support and end-of-life dates. For each version, extract: Glue version, Spark version, Python version, support end date, and status.

athena.name: Amazon Athena
athena.extraction_focus: Locate Athena engine version lifecycle information. Extract all engine versions with their lifecycle dates and deprecation status. For each version, extract: engine version, release date, deprecation date, and features.

redshift.name: Amazon Redshift
redshift.extraction_focus: Locate Redshift cluster version and maintenance track information. Extract deprecated node types and cluster versions with end of support dates. For each item, extract: version or node type, deprecation date, end of support date, and migration target.

item_properties.name: Redshift version or node type (e.g., 'Redshift dc1.large')

sagemaker.name: Amazon SageMaker
sagemaker.extraction_focus: Locate SageMaker framework and instance lifecycle information. Extract deprecated ML framework versions, deprecated instance types, and end-of-support notices. For each item, extract: framework name, version, deprecation date, and replacement.

comprehend.name: Amazon Comprehend
comprehend.extraction_focus: Locate Comprehend API version and model version lifecycle information. Extract deprecated API versions and model versions with their end of support dates. For each version, extract: version identifier, feature area, deprecation date, and status.

rekognition.name: Amazon Rekognition
rekognition.extraction_focus: Locate Rekognition model version lifecycle information. Extract deprecated face detection models, content moderation models, and API versions. For each version, extract: model name, version, deprecation date, and recommended migration.

item_properties.name: Rekognition model (e.g., 'Face Detection Model v6')
item_properties.model_type: Model type (Face Detection, Content Moderation, etc.)

waf.name: AWS WAF
waf.extraction_focus: Locate WAF Classic to WAFv2 migration information and deprecation notices. Extract deprecated WAF Classic features, rule group versions, and API versions. For each item, extract: feature name, deprecation date, migration deadline, and status.

guardduty.name: Amazon GuardDuty
guardduty.extraction_focus: Locate GuardDuty feature and runtime agent version lifecycle information. Extract deprecated protection plans, agent versions, and finding type changes. For each item, extract: feature name, version, deprecation date, and status.

item_properties.name: GuardDuty feature or version (e.g., 'GuardDuty Runtime Agent 1.0')
item_properties.feature_type: Feature type (Runtime Monitoring, Protection Plan, etc.)

inspector.name: Amazon Inspector
inspector.extraction_focus: Locate Inspector version lifecycle information, particularly the migration from Inspector Classic to Inspector v2. Extract deprecated assessment templates, agent versions, and Classic features. For each item, extract: feature name, version, deprecation date, and migration target.

elasticsearch.name: Amazon Elasticsearch Service (Legacy)
elasticsearch.extraction_focus: Locate Elasticsearch version lifecycle information for the legacy Amazon Elasticsearch Service domains. Extract all deprecated Elasticsearch versions and their migration paths to OpenSearch. For each version, extract: version, end of support date, and migration target.

kinesis.name: Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics
kinesis.extraction_focus: Locate Kinesis Data Analytics (now Managed Apache Flink) runtime version lifecycle. Extract Apache Flink runtime versions supported, with their deprecation and end of support dates. For each version, extract: Flink version, release date, end of support date, and status.

amplify.name: AWS Amplify
amplify.extraction_focus: Locate Amplify build image and framework version lifecycle information. Extract deprecated build images, Node.js versions, and framework versions. For each item, extract: build image or framework name, version, deprecation date, and replacement.

item_properties.name: Amplify build image or framework version (e.g., 'Amplify Build Image Amazon Linux 2')

codebuild.name: AWS CodeBuild
codebuild.extraction_focus: Locate CodeBuild build image and runtime version lifecycle information. Extract deprecated build images and runtime versions. For each item, extract: image name, runtime name, version, deprecation date, and replacement image.
